title: Requirements in Practice
shortSummary: Build stronger requirements in practice judgment so product work becomes clearer, more repeatable, and easier to lead.
whyItMatters: Requirements in Practice is a leverage point inside product. It supports the broader goal of what to build, what not to build, and how to ship it.
coreConcepts: Requirements in Practice needs clear definitions before it can become a reliable operating system.
Good requirements in practice work depends on better signal, better sequencing, and cleaner ownership.
Requirements in Practice should reduce rework and improve the quality of decisions across the business.
